homepage.heroSubline: Built to last in Colorado's toughest climate. We pour foundations, driveways, and patios that stand up to Denver's freeze-thaw cycles.
aboutText: Apex Concrete started with one truck and a commitment to honest work. Twelve years later, we're still family-owned, serving Denver homes with the same integrity.
We don't cut corners. Our crews use reinforced steel and proper grading techniques to prevent the cracking that plagues Colorado concrete.
When we're not pouring concrete, you'll find us sponsoring local Little League teams and volunteering with Habitat for Humanity builds across the Front Range.

valueProps.title: Weather-Tested
valueProps.description: Our mixes include freeze-thaw resistant additives proven in Denver's harsh winters.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: We know exactly how soil composition varies from Capitol Hill to Highlands Ranch.

valueProps.title: No Surprises
valueProps.description: Fixed-price quotes include compaction testing and city permit fees.

valueProps.title: Fast Cleanup
valueProps.description: Job sites are broom-clean before we leave - no concrete splatter on your historic brickwork.

homepage.ctaSubline: Get a same-week assessment with one of our lead concrete specialists.

services.name: Driveway Installation
services.shortDescription: Heavy-duty driveways built to resist Colorado's freeze-thaw damage.
services.description:
Denver's temperature swings destroy weak concrete. We pour 6-inch reinforced driveways with expansion joints spaced every 10 feet - critical for preventing the cracks you see across older Lakewood homes.

Our process starts with excavating 12 inches below grade to remove unstable soil. We compact in layers with a vibratory plate, then add wire mesh and fiber reinforcement before pouring. For steep driveways near Red Rocks, we include traction grooves to prevent icy slip hazards.

Expect 3-5 days from tear-out to final cure. We'll coordinate with your HOA if you're in a historic district like Park Hill.
process: Step 1: Mark utility lines and grade for proper drainage away from your foundation
Step 2: Excavate and compact subbase with native gravel
Step 3: Install formwork with expansion joints every 10 feet
Step 4: Pour 4000 PSI air-entrained concrete with fiber additives
benefits: Eliminates standing water that causes icy patches
Reinforcement prevents cracking from heavy trucks
Custom finishes: exposed aggregate, stamped patterns
Meets Denver residential code for slope and thickness

faq.question: How soon can I park on my new driveway?
faq.answer: Wait 7 days for passenger vehicles. We'll provide a cure timeline based on weather - summer pours set faster than winter installations.

faq.question: Do you handle permits?
faq.answer: Yes. Denver requires permits for any driveway replacement. We include this in our quote and file the paperwork.

services.name: Foundation Repair
services.shortDescription: Stabilize settling foundations in Denver's clay-heavy soils.
services.description:
Colorado's expansive clay soils shift dramatically with moisture changes, causing the foundation cracks common in 1950s Arvada ranch homes. We assess damage with laser levels before recommending solutions.

For minor settling, we inject polyurethane foam to lift slabs evenly. Severe cases require steel push piers driven to stable bedrock - a specialty when working near the South Platte River's unstable banks.

Repairs typically take 2-4 days. We'll explain how proper gutter drainage and soil grading can prevent future issues, especially in neighborhoods like Wash Park with mature tree roots near foundations.
process: Step 1: Laser-level scan to map settlement patterns
Step 2: Excavate access points for piers or foam injection
Step 3: Install support system with 25-year warranty
Step 4: Backfill and restore landscaping
benefits: Meets engineering standards for Colorado soils

faq.question: How do I know if my foundation needs repair?
faq.answer: Look for 1/4"+ cracks in basement walls, uneven floors, or gaps around window frames. We offer free inspections with moisture meter testing.

faq.question: Will this affect my home's appraisal?
faq.answer: Properly documented repairs often increase value. We provide engineer-certified reports for your records.

services.name: Concrete Patios
services.shortDescription: Custom outdoor living spaces designed for Denver's climate.
services.description:
Turn your backyard into an entertainment hub with a concrete patio built for Colorado weather. We design with proper slope to shed hail runoff, and use vapor barriers to prevent efflorescence stains common in Littleton's mineral-rich soil.

Choose from brushed finishes for traction after snow melts, or upgrade to stamped patterns resembling flagstone. For modern RiNo homes, we integrate sleek exposed aggregate with radiant heat cables - perfect for extending patio season into chilly fall evenings.

Projects typically complete in 5-7 days. We'll advise on optimal placement to avoid shadowing from the Front Range mountains during prime BBQ hours.
process: Step 1: Site plan with measurements for furniture/grill zones
Step 2: Excavate and grade with 2% slope away from house
Step 3: Install insulated forms for heated options
Step 4: Pour and finish with your selected texture

faq.question: Can you match my existing concrete?
faq.answer: We'll take samples to our supplier for custom tint matching within 90% accuracy - critical for historic district compliance.

faq.question: What's the ideal thickness?
faq.answer: 4 inches minimum for foot traffic. We recommend 5 inches if you'll have hot tubs or outdoor kitchens.

services.name: Sidewalk Replacement
services.shortDescription: ADA-compliant walkways that survive Denver winters.
services.description:
Tree roots and ice damage create trip hazards on older Denver sidewalks. We replace sections to meet city ADA standards while preserving mature landscaping in neighborhoods like Capitol Hill.

Our crews work efficiently to minimize disruption - we'll coordinate with your neighbors if replacing shared walkways. For commercial properties, we schedule pours during off-hours to maintain customer access.

Expect 2-3 days per 20 linear feet. We use fiber-reinforced concrete with control joints every 4 feet, preventing the alligator cracking common near bus stops and school routes.
process: Step 1: Mark utilities and obtain city right-of-way permits
Step 2: Saw-cut and remove damaged sections
Step 3: Grade for proper drainage (1/4" per foot)
Step 4: Pour slip-resistant broom finish

faq.question: Who pays for sidewalk repairs?
faq.answer: Denver property owners are responsible for adjacent sidewalks. We help navigate the city's 50/50 cost-sharing program for eligible repairs.

faq.question: Can you work around tree roots?
faq.Answer: Yes. We'll consult an arborist for prized shade trees, sometimes rerouting the walkway instead of cutting major roots.

services.name: Garage Floors
services.shortDescription: Industrial-grade garage slabs for mountain living.
services.description:
Colorado garages take abuse from snow melt, road salt, and heavy gear. We pour 5-inch slabs with vapor barriers and steel fibers - the same mix used in Vail resort parking structures.

Epoxy coatings are available in Denver Broncos colors or simple gray. For workshop areas, we add silica sand for traction when boots are wet from spring slush.

Most residential garages take 2 days. We'll time the pour to avoid afternoon thunderstorms common east of I-25, ensuring proper curing.
process: Step 1: Pressure-wash existing slab (if resurfacing)
Step 2: Diamond grind for maximum coating adhesion
Step 3: Apply two-part epoxy with UV inhibitors
Step 4: Add decorative flakes or traction additives
benefits: Resists oil stains from ski/snowboard tuning
Brightens dark spaces with reflective coatings
Easier to sweep out winter gravel
Withstands jack stands and heavy tool drops

faq.question: How long before I can park on it?
faq.Answer: 72 hours for coatings. New slabs require 7-day cure time before vehicle loads.

faq.question: Will salt damage the finish?
faq.Answer: Our commercial-grade epoxy resists chloride damage better than bare concrete. We recommend rinsing monthly in winter.

services.name: Concrete Stairs
services.shortDescription: Safe, durable steps for Denver's hilly lots.
services.description:
Steep property grades across Denver demand sturdy stairs. We build monolithic concrete steps with 11-inch treads and 7-inch risers - the ideal proportions for navigating icy mornings.

Rebar cages tie into existing foundations for stability. For historic homes near Cheesman Park, we replicate original profiles while adding modern non-slip finishes.

Typical 5-step installations take 3 days. We form curved designs for contemporary properties, or straight runs to match mid-century modern aesthetics in Krisana Heights.
process: Step 1: Survey elevation changes with transit
Step 2: Form treads/risers to local building code
Step 3: Pour integral color concrete
Step 4: Apply abrasive strips for winter safety

faq.question: Can you replace just one step?
faq.Answer: We recommend full replacements for structural integrity. Partial repairs often fail within two freeze-thaw cycles.

faq.question: Do you need engineering plans?
faq.Answer: Only for flights over 30 inches tall. Most Denver residential jobs fall under standard building codes.

services.name: Concrete Retaining Walls
services.shortDescription: Hold back Colorado's shifting soils with engineered walls.
services.description:
Denver's terrain requires serious retaining solutions. We build reinforced concrete walls up to 6 feet tall without engineer approval - perfect for tiered landscaping in Highlands Ranch hillside properties.

Every wall includes weep holes and gravel backfill to handle our heavy spring rains. For visible front-yard walls, we offer form-lined finishes that resemble natural stone without the maintenance of actual boulders.

Allow 1-2 weeks depending on wall length. We coordinate with landscapers to ensure proper drainage behind the wall, preventing the bulges seen in older Cherry Creek neighborhood installations.
process: Step 1: Dig footer trench below frost line (36" in Denver)
Step 2: Set rebar grid with vertical supports every 4 feet
Step 3: Pour concrete in lifts for proper consolidation
Step 4: Install drainage system with filter fabric

faq.question: How long do these walls last?
faq.Answer: Properly built concrete walls typically outlast the homeowner. We warranty structural integrity for 25 years.

faq.question: Can you match my house's exterior?
faq.Answer: Yes. Acid stains or integral colors can approximate brick, stucco, or natural stone tones.

reviews.name: Mark T.
reviews.text: Apex replaced our cracked 1980s driveway in Lakewood with a reinforced 6-inch slab. Two winters later - not a single crack despite the heavy plow traffic. Crew cleaned up every bit of concrete splatter from our historic brick porch.

reviews.name: Lisa H.
reviews.text: The team stabilized our settling Arvada ranch home with helical piers. No more sticking doors, and they preserved our prized blue spruce roots during excavation. Detailed engineering report helped with our refinance appraisal.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: Our new heated patio in RiNo gets used year-round. The crew suggested adding radiant cables during the pour - best decision ever for Denver winters. Finish matches our industrial loft's concrete floors perfectly.

reviews.name: Danielle K.
reviews.text: Apex handled all the permits and city inspections for our Capitol Hill sidewalk replacement. They worked around our 100-year-old maple tree without damaging roots. Neighbors keep asking who did the work.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: Epoxy coating in Broncos orange and blue transformed my dingy garage. Even after spilling transmission fluid during snowmobile maintenance, it wiped right up. Crew finished a day early despite April snow showers.

reviews.name: Sarah L.
reviews.text: The 4-foot wall in our Highlands Ranch backyard stopped the erosion that was threatening our deck. Form-lined finish looks like natural sandstone but without the upkeep. Project manager personally checked on curing during that freak May hailstorm.

service-areas.title: Denver Area Concrete Services
service-areas.description: Local concrete contractors serving Aurora, Lakewood, Arvada, Littleton, Highlands Ranch and all Denver metro neighborhoods.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Concrete proudly serves Aurora, Colorado, a bustling city with a population of approximately 395,000 residents. Known for its 1970s-80s suburban developments, Aurora offers a mix of residential and commercial concrete needs. Whether you're looking to repair aging driveways or install new patios, our team understands the challenges posed by the area's cold winters and heavy snowfall. We specialize in durable solutions that withstand Aurora's harsh climate, ensuring long-lasting results for homeowners and businesses alike.
serviceAreas.localContext: Aurora features sprawling suburban neighborhoods with many homes built in the 1970s and 1980s, requiring frequent concrete maintenance due to freeze-thaw cycles.

serviceAreas.content: Lakewood, Colorado, with its population of around 157,000, is home to many 1960s-70s split-level homes that often require concrete repairs or upgrades. Apex Concrete provides expert services for driveways, sidewalks, and foundations, tailored to withstand the area's cold winters and heavy snow. Our team is familiar with Lakewood's architectural styles and can seamlessly match existing concrete work for cohesive results. Trust us to enhance your property's durability and curb appeal.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lakewood's split-level homes from the 1960s-70s often feature concrete foundations and driveways that need reinforcement due to seasonal weather stress.

serviceAreas.content: Arvada, Colorado, is a charming city with approximately 124,000 residents, known for its 1960s-70s ranch-style homes. Apex Concrete specializes in repairing and replacing aging concrete surfaces, such as driveways and patios, which are common in these older neighborhoods. Our team understands the impact of Arvada's cold winters and heavy snow on concrete structures and uses high-quality materials to ensure longevity. Whether you need a new foundation or decorative concrete, we deliver reliable solutions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Arvada's ranch-style homes often feature expansive concrete driveways and walkways that require maintenance due to decades of wear and weather exposure.

serviceAreas.content: Littleton, Colorado, with its population of roughly 48,000, boasts a historic downtown filled with bungalows and older homes. Apex Concrete provides tailored solutions for Littleton's unique concrete needs, from repairing cracked sidewalks to installing stamped concrete patios. The area's cold winters and heavy snowfall demand durable materials, and our team ensures every project meets the highest standards. Let us help you preserve Littleton's charm while upgrading your property's functionality.
serviceAreas.localContext: Littleton's historic bungalows often feature original concrete work that needs restoration to maintain both aesthetics and structural integrity.

serviceAreas.content: Highlands Ranch, Colorado, is a master-planned community with around 105,000 residents, featuring homes built primarily in the 1990s-2000s. Apex Concrete offers modern concrete solutions, including decorative finishes and durable driveways, to complement the area's newer construction. Despite the community's relatively recent development, the cold winters and heavy snow still take a toll on concrete surfaces. Our team ensures your concrete projects are built to last, enhancing both beauty and functionality.
serviceAreas.localContext: Highlands Ranch's planned community homes often incorporate contemporary concrete designs that benefit from professional maintenance and upgrades.
